content: I have reviewed your claim regarding the dental injury sustained on May 30th, 2024. Please provide the following evidence: a copy of the restaurant receipt, any written incident report or names of staff notified, and your complete dental records including X-rays and itemized invoices for the $4,500 treatment.

Good morning your honor. I have the documents right here. This is the dental invoice for the extracion totaling $4500, and here
 is the receipt and statement confirming my visit to central east asian cuisine on may 30th, 2024.

The defense respectfully denies liability as bones are a natural and expected component of a 'Big Plate Whole Chicken' dish, meaning the plaintiff assumed the inherent risk of consumption. Furthermore, the plaintiff has failed to provide medical evidence establishing that the tooth fracture was caused exclusively by this meal rather than a pre-existing condition.

I have reviewed the receipt and dental invoice in turn_1_1_evidence_1.png, but I need medical documentation linking the injury specifically to this incident. Please provide the dentist's clinical notes regarding the cause of the fracture and the names of the staff members you notified at the restaurant. Evidence types needed: clinical dental records, witness statements.

The defense notes that the clinical report characterizes the cause as a 'non-food object,' yet bones are an inherent and expected component of a 'Whole Chicken' dish, making their presence an assumed risk for any consumer. Furthermore, the plaintiff has failed to provide the requested names of the staff notified, and the dental invoices submitted are largely illegible and appear to lack the authenticity required for professional documentation.

The clinical report in turn_4_1_evidence_3.png identifies the cause of your injury as a 'non-food object,' which contradicts your initial claim that you bit into 'small bones.' Please provide [a clarification regarding the specific object you bit and written witness statements from the two friends who were present at the meal]. Evidence types needed: witness statements, clarified statement of fact.

The defense maintains that a bone is a natural and expected component of a 'Whole Chicken' dish, and the significant size of the object in the photo suggests the plaintiff failed to exercise reasonable care. Furthermore, the plaintiff has failed to provide the requested witness statements or explain the discrepancy in the clinical report, which attributes the injury to a 'non-food object' rather than a bone.

I acknowledge the photograph of the bone in turn_7_1_evidence_2.png, yet you have still failed to provide the witness statements from your companions or address the 'non-food object' description in your medical report. Please provide [written witness statements from the two friends present and a clarification as to why the dentist in turn_4_1_evidence_3.png characterized the object as 'non-food']. Evidence types needed: witness statements, clarified statement of fact.
I do not have any witness statement I am sorry

The Plaintiff’s admission that no witness statements exist leaves the claim entirely unsubstantiated, failing to meet the necessary burden of proof. Furthermore, the defense maintains that bones are an inherent risk in a 'Whole Chicken' dish, and the Plaintiff has failed to reconcile the clinical report’s description of a 'non-food object' with their current allegation.

The Court finds in favor of the Defendant and awards $0.00. While the photograph in turn_7_1_evidence_2.png shows a bone, the Plaintiff failed to provide the requested witness statements or resolve the critical discrepancy in the clinical report (turn_4_1_evidence_3.png), which attributes the injury to a 'non-food object.' Furthermore, under Illinois law, consumers must reasonably expect bones in a dish explicitly titled 'Whole Chicken,' and the Plaintiff has not met the burden of proof to establish liability.
